title: RC Mini Forklift — Scissor Lift and Drive Mechanism
design_question: How to build an RC-controlled mini forklift with motorised scissor lift and pallet forks?
selected_approach: Chassis: 4-wheel differential drive (4× N20 gear motors, 12 V). Scissor lift: 2-stage aluminium scissor linkage driven by a 12 V worm-gear DC motor + lead screw (2 mm pitch). Fork width 150 mm, lift height 0–250 mm. NRF24L01 2.4 GHz RF link for RC control. Arduino Nano: receives joystick data (drive + lift direction), PWM-drives L298N for drive wheels and lift motor.
rationale: Worm gear prevents scissor from back-driving under load (self-locking). Lead screw gives 5 mm/rev movement with adequate force. N20 motors in differential drive achieve low-profile chassis for pallet entry. NRF24L01 provides reliable 100 m range.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Pump and cylinder adds complexity and weight.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Scissor Lift / Pantograph — Mechanical Advantage and Geometry' confirms F_lift = F_h/(2n·tan θ).
supporting_fact_titles: Scissor Lift / Pantograph — Mechanical Advantage and Geometry
Worm Gear + Lead Screw Linear Actuator
